opp.name: Alcoa [S2P] 2026
opp.next_step: GN - Team met with them at APC - New Orleans. They are in the market. Setting up a call with them ASAP
opp.ais_why:
• No Avoma calls available for this opportunity. SF description is sparse: team met at APC in New Orleans, Alcoa is replacing SAP, currently uses Ariba and Oracle, 'immediate' initiative.
• No mention of Merlin, ANA, AP Smart Desk, agentic, or any specific AI use case in the opp record. AIS_Score is null. Conversation is a deep-dive setup, not an AI vision discussion.
• Just created late March 2026 with last activity in May. Real deal motion but zero AI signal yet - default lower curious until first discovery call surfaces specifics.

sources: Salesforce quantified-value fields unavailable or empty
metrics.narrative: No quantified business problem or ROI case has been documented in Salesforce fields (Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c, Value_to_Customer__c all unread) and no Avoma calls have been matched to this opportunity to extract buyer-stated pain or financial impact. The deal is very early (91 days old, in Qualified stage) and the NextStep indicates the team just met the prospect at APC New Orleans and is scheduling the first substantive call. At this stage the absence of quantified metrics is expected; discovery has not yet occurred. The immediate move is to run a structured discovery call that surfaces the operational pain (contract cycle time, maverick spend, invoice processing cost, supplier management burden) and quantifies at least one dimension (FTE savings, cycle-time reduction, error-rate improvement, or cost avoidance).

sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: 4 contacts, none tagged Champion
Salesforce champion fields unavailable or empty
champion.narrative: No champion has been identified through contact roles (none tagged Champion), Salesforce fields (X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c unread), or Avoma calls (none matched). Four contacts are on record: Mateus Tiraboschi (VP, Decision Maker), Ronna Hofer (Director, Influencer), Nancy Provencher (Director, Influencer), and the deal owner's manager (Manager). Tiraboschi's Decision Maker tag and VP title suggest authority, but no evidence shows he is advocating internally for Zycus, providing intel on competitors, or driving the agenda. The deal is very early (just met at APC, first call pending per NextStep 24 Jun), so no champion relationship has developed yet. The next move is to identify on the upcoming calls who is driving the S2P initiative internally, who has the most to gain (the pain owner), and who has access to the Economic Buyer or CFO. Build rapport with that person, provide them value (peer benchmarks, ROI models, competitive intel), and track whether they advocate for Zycus in internal discussions.

sources: Salesforce competition synthesis fields unavailable or empty
competition.narrative: No competitors have been documented in Salesforce (Competitors__c field is 'unknown' per ground truth, and competition synthesis fields Others_Competitors_Please_specify__c, How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c, Existing_vendor__c, Replacing_What__c all unread) and no Avoma calls exist to capture buyer mentions of rivals or incumbent systems. The deal is early (91 days old, Qualified stage, first substantive call pending per NextStep) so competitive landscape has not been mapped. For a global enterprise S2P deal, expect competition from the relevant stakeholder (incumbent in many large manufacturing orgs), Coupa (SaaS peer), GEP (AI-forward peer), Jaggaer, or an incumbent legacy system (Oracle, homegrown). The immediate move is to ask on the first discovery call: what are you using today for contracts, sourcing, procurement, and invoicing; are you evaluating other vendors; and what is driving you to change now. Map the competitive field early and position Zycus Merlin AI as the wedge.

sources: SF NextStep 24 Jun 2026: 'They are in the market'
Salesforce pain fields unavailable or empty
identify_pain.narrative: No operational or financial pain has been documented in Salesforce (Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c, What_if_this_is_not_done__c, Compelling_Event__c unread) and no Avoma calls exist to capture buyer-stated pain. The NextStep confirms the prospect is 'in the market' (stated at APC New Orleans meet, late June 2026), which signals active buying intent and implies dissatisfaction with their current state, but the specific pain (contract bottlenecks, maverick spend leakage, invoice processing cost, supplier risk, lack of spend visibility, manual sourcing) has not been named or quantified. The deal is early (first substantive call pending), so this gap is expected. The immediate move is to run a pain-discovery call that surfaces: what is broken today, what is the cost of the current state (FTE burden, cycle time, error rate, compliance risk), who owns the pain (procurement ops, finance, legal, category management), and what forcing function is driving them to act now (contract expiration, system sunset, cost-reduction mandate, merger integration).

sources: Salesforce paper-process fields unavailable or empty
paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics (MSA, SOW, legal review, procurement channel, InfoSec review, ERP integration scope) have been documented in Salesforce and no Avoma calls exist to surface Alcoa's paper process. The deal is in early Qualified stage (91 days old, first substantive call pending per NextStep), so contracting has not been discussed. For a $250k S2P suite deal with a global enterprise like Alcoa, expect a formal MSA/SOW negotiation, InfoSec review (data residency, SSO, penetration testing), legal redlines (liability caps, data ownership, SLA penalties), and ERP integration scoping (likely SAP or Oracle integration for PR/PO/invoice flow). The next move is to surface on the upcoming discovery call whether Alcoa has a preferred vendor contracting template, what their InfoSec review cycle looks like, and who leads legal/procurement operations (to map the contract owner early).

sources: Salesforce EB fields unavailable or empty
economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer has been identified through contact roles (none with EB or Executive Sponsor role), Salesforce fields (X10a_Sponsor__c, Who_will_approve_budget__c unread), or Avoma calls (none matched). Mateus Tiraboschi (VP of Global Procurement & Transportation) is tagged Decision Maker in contact roles, which suggests exec-level access, but no call or field evidence confirms he controls the commercial decision or budget approval. For a $250k S2P suite deal the EB is likely at VP Procurement or CFO level; Tiraboschi's VP title makes him a candidate, but his authority over budget and final approval has not been validated. The deal is early (just post-APC meet, first call pending) so this gap is expected. The next move is to ask explicitly on the upcoming discovery call who approves the spend and what the approval chain looks like, and to request an introduction to that person.

sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: 4 contacts spanning VP, Director, Manager levels and multiple geographies
Salesforce process fields unavailable or empty
decision_process.narrative: The stakeholder structure is partially visible: four contacts are on record (Mateus Tiraboschi VP Global Procurement tagged Decision Maker, Ronna Hofer Director tagged Influencer, Nancy Provencher Director Procurement Canada tagged Influencer, Marcos Reis Global Project Procurement Manager), suggesting a multi-geography, multi-function buying committee typical of a global enterprise. However, no approval chain, timeline, or formal process steps (RFI, RFP, shortlist, POC, board approval) have been documented in Salesforce (What_is_the_decision_process__c, Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c unread) and no Avoma calls exist to describe how Alcoa runs vendor selection. The deal is early (just met at APC, first call pending), so the process has not been mapped yet. The next move is to ask on the upcoming call: what is your evaluation timeline, who needs to approve this spend, are you running an RFP or a direct evaluation, and when do you need a solution live. Map the process explicitly and anchor the close date to their go-live forcing function.

sources: Salesforce criteria fields unavailable or empty
decision_criteria.narrative: No decision criteria or evaluation framework has been documented in Salesforce (Business_Requirements__c, Top_Challenges_Priorities__c, AI_Needs_in_RFP_Rating__c unread) and no Avoma calls exist to extract buyer-stated priorities, BRD fit points, or test-case validation criteria. The deal is in early Qualified stage (91 days old) and the NextStep confirms the first substantive engagement (APC meet, pending call setup), so no formal RFP or structured evaluation has occurred yet. At this stage the absence of criteria is expected. The upcoming discovery call should surface what the buyer will evaluate: system uptime, user adoption/ease-of-use, integration with their ERP (likely SAP or Oracle given Alcoa's scale), contract authoring speed, AI-driven sourcing recommendations, spend visibility, and compliance tracking. Once criteria emerge, map Zycus strengths (Merlin AI negotiation, unified S2P platform) directly to them.

Deal is live and moving post-APC touch.

confidence_signals.summary: Deal is live and moving: last activity 24 Jun 2026 (2 days ago), NextStep confirms recent in-person APC engagement and prospect stated they are 'in the market', follow-up call being scheduled ASAP. Four-contact buying committee spanning VP/Director/Manager levels and multiple geographies (Global, Canada) suggests enterprise-scale engagement. Products scoped (full S2P suite: iContract, iSource, eProcurement, eInvoicing) and Decision Maker role assigned. No cooling signals observed. Confidence is currently limited by early-stage gaps (no discovery call yet, no MEDDPICC coverage, no calls matched), but engagement trajectory is positive.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 27 Mar 2026 (91 days ago), qualified immediately, last activity 24 Jun (2 days ago). Close date 31 Mar 2027 is 278 days out. Time-in-Qualified: 91 days. For a $250k enterprise S2P deal with a 12-15 month typical cycle, current pace is appropriate for this stage. No slip history observed. NextStep confirms recent in-person contact at APC conference and prospect is actively in-market.

north_star_verdict.headline: Early-stage $250k S2P deal progressing appropriately; team connected with prospect at APC New Orleans on 24-Jun, confirmed in-market status, ASAP discovery call being scheduled. 278 days to close aligns with enterprise procurement cycle.

best_practice_check.summary: Early-stage deal (91 days old, Qualified) with strong setup opportunity: four-contact multi-geography buying committee, full S2P product scope, and recent in-person APC engagement confirming active buying intent. Immediate priorities: run structured discovery to map pain/metrics/EB/process, multi-thread across the four known contacts, and position Merlin AI early as the competitive wedge.

competitive_position.summary: Competitive landscape is unknown. No competitors documented in Salesforce (Competitors__c field reported as 'unknown') and no Avoma calls matched to extract buyer mentions of rivals or incumbent systems. The deal is 91 days old, in early Qualified stage, and the first substantive engagement (APC meet, pending call) just occurred on 24 Jun 2026, so competitive discovery has not yet happened. For a $250k S2P suite deal with a global manufacturing enterprise like Alcoa, expect competition from SAP Ariba (likely incumbent given Alcoa's scale and industry), Coupa (SaaS peer), GEP (AI-forward peer), or a legacy Oracle/homegrown system. The immediate move is to map the competitive field on the first discovery call: ask what they use today, whether they are evaluating other vendors, and what is driving the change. Position Zycus Merlin AI negotiation agent and unified S2P platform as the wedge once the field is known.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI positioning is unmapped. No AIS score, status, or why fields are populated (all null per ground truth) and no Avoma calls exist to assess whether the Merlin AI narrative has been introduced or is landing. Products scoped include iContract, iSource, eProcurement, eInvoicing (full S2P suite) but no explicit Merlin or AI product flag. The deal is early (first call pending), so AI positioning has not occurred. For a global enterprise procurement deal, AI-driven sourcing recommendations, contract intelligence, and negotiation agents are high-leverage differentiators. Next move: ask on the first discovery call whether AI capabilities are in their evaluation criteria and introduce Merlin as the competitive wedge if they signal appetite.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Customer expectations and fit criteria are unmapped. No business requirements, evaluation priorities, or fit signals have been documented in Salesforce and no Avoma calls exist. The deal is early (first call pending post-APC meet). Expected enterprise S2P criteria include: ERP integration depth (Alcoa likely runs SAP or Oracle), user adoption/ease-of-use across geographies, contract authoring speed, AI-driven sourcing recommendations (savings visibility), spend analytics and compliance tracking, InfoSec/data residency requirements (global footprint), and implementation timeline. Products scoped (iContract, iSource, eProcurement, eInvoicing) suggest full S2P suite fit. Next move: surface buyer priorities on discovery call and map Zycus strengths to them.

deltas.subject: Multi-thread immediately across the four known contacts (Mateus Tiraboschi VP, Ronna Hofer Director, Nancy Provencher Director Canada, Marcos Reis Manager) to build redundancy and map internal dynamics. Alcoa is a global enterprise with complex procurement governance; relying on a single thread (likely Tiraboschi as Decision Maker) risks ghost if that person disengages. Engage each contact individually to understand their role in the evaluation, their priorities, and their influence, and identify who is the internal champion (the pain owner most motivated to fix the current state).

value.flag: Multi-thread immediately across the four known contacts (Mateus Tiraboschi VP, Ronna Hofer Director, Nancy Provencher Director Canada, Marcos Reis Manager) to build redundancy and map internal dynamics. Alcoa is a global enterprise with complex procurement governance; relying on a single thread (likely Tiraboschi as Decision Maker) risks ghost if that person disengages. value.flag: Position Zycus Merlin AI negotiation agent and unified S2P platform as the competitive wedge from the first discovery call forward. For a global manufacturing enterprise like Alcoa, AI-driven sourcing recommendations, contract intelligence, and spend visibility are high-value differentiators against legacy systems (Ariba, Oracle) and SaaS peers (Coupa, GEP). Ask on the discovery call whether AI capabilities are in their evaluation criteria (reference AI_Needs_in_RFP_Rating__c once available) and anchor Merlin as the Phase 1 centerpiece if they signal AI appetite.
